must_find.description: Holding funds requires ALL of front+fund+principal routed to wash; setting only principal still releases the full amount as a live wire. The dangerous outcome (wire out) is the default; safety requires a non-obvious all-of configuration.

The confirmation warning fires but shows neither amount nor intent (interest vs principal), so it cannot distinguish the safe payment from the catastrophic one — a guard that exists but does not guard.

must_not_claim.description: BITE-RISK. The flaw is default direction and conjunctive suppression, not operator error. A review must not conclude the fix is better docs/training/manuals.
A review must not claim the existing confirmation warning is adequate mitigation — its presence is exactly the false comfort the incident exposed.
